questions.text: Why does the Surah begin with many oaths (sun, moon, day, night, sky, earth, soul)?
questions.answer: To strongly confirm the message: the soul’s purification and corruption have real consequences.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that people are morally neutral and have no inner awareness of right and wrong.
questions.answer: False. It teaches the soul is inspired with awareness of both wickedness and righteousness.

questions.text: What does the contrast of day and night teach about moral clarity?
questions.answer: Just as light reveals and darkness covers, truth and sin affect the soul’s clarity.

questions.text: What does it mean to purify the soul (tazkiyah) practically?
questions.answer: To cleanse the heart from shirk and sins, build taqwa, and train good character consistently.

questions.text: What is one sign that someone is corrupting their soul?
questions.answer: They repeatedly ignore conscience, justify sin, and become comfortable with ظلم and disobedience.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches success is accidental and unrelated to effort.
questions.answer: False. It links success to active purification of the soul.

questions.text: How can a believer strengthen the soul’s taqwa side daily?
questions.answer: By prayer, Quran, dhikr, avoiding major sins, and making sincere repentance quickly.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention Thamud as an example?
questions.answer: To show how arrogance and transgression lead to rejecting truth and suffering destruction.

questions.text: What moral lesson is taught by the story of the she-camel?
questions.answer: When people attack clear signs and oppress what Allah has honored, they invite severe punishment.

questions.text: What is one modern example of transgression like Thamud’s attitude?
questions.answer: Rejecting clear guidance out of pride and trying to silence reminders that challenge desires.

questions.text: How does this Surah teach accountability for community crimes?
questions.answer: Even if one person leads a crime, the society that supports or accepts it shares responsibility.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that Allah’s punishment is unjust or uncontrolled.
questions.answer: False. It presents punishment as a just response to sin and rebellion.

questions.text: What does it mean that Allah does not fear the consequence of His punishment?
questions.answer: Allah’s justice is complete and not limited by weakness or regret; He is the absolute Judge.

questions.text: How can someone avoid becoming like the ‘most wretched’ among Thamud?
questions.answer: Do not follow destructive leaders; listen to guidance, respect Allah’s limits, and repent.

questions.text: How does this Surah correct the idea that morality is only social convention?
questions.answer: It roots morality in the soul’s God-given awareness and in divine judgment.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that hiding sin removes its harm.
questions.answer: False. Corruption affects the soul even if others don’t see it.

questions.text: What is one daily self-audit practice inspired by this Surah?
questions.answer: Before sleep, review one sin to repent from and one good deed to continue.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach balance between hope and fear?
questions.answer: It offers hope through purification and success, and fear through corruption and destruction.

questions.text: What does the Surah imply about the danger of normalizing sin?
questions.answer: Persistent sin hardens the soul and makes denial easier, leading to failure.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that a person’s inner state is irrelevant if they appear good.
questions.answer: False. It centers success on the state of the soul.

questions.text: How many consecutive oaths does Allah take at the start of Surah Ash-Shams?
questions.answer: Seven oaths (Sun, Moon, Day, Night, Sky, Earth, and the Soul).

questions.text: What does 'Duhaha' mean in this Surah?
questions.answer: The brightness or morning light of the sun.

questions.text: What does the Surah say the Moon does in relation to the sun?
questions.answer: It follows the sun.

questions.text: How is the 'Day' described in relation to the sun?
questions.answer: As that which reveals or displays its glory.

questions.text: How is the 'Night' described in relation to the sun?
questions.answer: As that which covers or conceals it.

questions.text: What did Allah inspire the soul with according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Its wickedness (Fujur) and its righteousness (Taqwa).

questions.text: Who has succeeded according to this Surah?
questions.answer: He who has purified the soul (Zakkaha).

questions.text: Who has failed according to this Surah?
questions.answer: He who has corrupted/instilled it with sin (Dassaha).

questions.text: Which nation is used as an example of failure?
questions.answer: The nation of Thamud.

questions.text: Why did Thamud deny their Prophet?
questions.answer: Because of their 'transgression' (Taghwaha).

questions.text: Who was the 'most wretched' (Ashqaha) of Thamud?
questions.answer: The man (Qudar bin Salif) who stepped forward to kill the She-Camel.

questions.text: What was the command of the Messenger of Allah (Salih) regarding the She-Camel?
questions.answer: '[Do not harm] the she-camel of Allah or [prevent her from] her drink'.

questions.text: What did the people of Thamud do to the camel?
questions.answer: They denied him (the Prophet) and hamstrung/killed her.

questions.text: How did Allah punish Thamud?
questions.answer: He crushed them for their sin and leveled them.

questions.text: Does Allah fear the 'consequence' of His punishment?
questions.answer: No, He does not fear its consequence.

questions.text: What is the spiritual significance of the sun/moon contrast in this Surah?
questions.answer: It reflects the dual nature of the human soul (good and evil) and the clarity of guidance.

questions.text: What does 'Tahlaha' mean in this Surah?
questions.answer: How Allah spread or expanded the earth.

questions.text: Is the ability to choose between good and evil innate to humans?
questions.answer: Yes, Allah inspired the soul with awareness of both right and wrong.

questions.text: What is the 'She-Camel of Allah'?
questions.answer: A miraculous sign sent to Thamud that was directly attributed to Allah's power.

questions.text: What is the main takeaway regarding the soul?
questions.answer: Success is not accidental; it is the result of active purification (Tazkiyah).
